coachingItemsStructured.body: conversation about solo queue vs. stack performance discrepancy

Ash (65r / 38.5%) is the volume pick but underperforming relative to season expectations. Brava (33r / 54.5%) is outperforming Ash by a wide margin with a meaningful sample. Worth experimenting with Brava as the default ATK pick on maps where DEF gadget destruction is valuable, rather than defaulting to Ash every round. Also note Twitch (55r / 43.6%) — confirm the team is getting value from the drone-disable intel role, not just using it as a generic soft-breacher. Caveira DEF (90r / 64.4%) is elite — protect that role.

All top-6 ATK ops are underperforming: Thermite (40r / 35%), Osa (38r / 36.8%), Nomad (28r / 32.1%), Fuze (23r / 39.1%), Thatcher (22r / 41.4%), Zero (22r / 45.5%). Rauora (15r / 53.3%) and Gridlock (11r / 72.7%) are the clear bright spots. Consider shifting volume toward Rauora as a primary ATK pick — the sample is meaningful and the WR gap is significant. ATK improvement is the biggest lever for Hound's overall performance.

teamFocusItems.text: Attack win rate is the gap.
teamFocusItems.body: The team wins on defense and loses rounds on attack — all 5 players have strong DEF stats. Every session, identify at least one ATK-side adjustment (timing, formation, or operator) to close the gap. Even a 5–10% improvement in ATK WR translates directly to rank gains.

teamFocusItems.text: Protect your best maps in the ban phase.
teamFocusItems.body: Clubhouse, Chalet, and Border are the current strength maps. Don't give them up in the ban phase. Let the opponent ban their own bad maps — your job is to protect your wins.

teamFocusItems.text: Entry timing is a team action, not a solo one.
teamFocusItems.body: Peej leads entry with Ash — but entry only works when breach detonation and utility are deployed first. Hard breach goes before Ash pushes. If Peej is dying on entry, ask whether breach/utility preceded the push.

When callouts break down, each player makes defensive reads in isolation and the defense collapses. Prioritize active comms through plant phase and post-plant. The team's DEF strength comes from coordination, not just individual anchors.

teamFocusItems.text: Emerald Plains is a hard ban — no exceptions.
teamFocusItems.body: Universal 0% WR across all five players. Do not negotiate it away in the ban phase regardless of opponent tendencies. This is the team's unambiguous worst map.

teamFocusItems.text: Oregon and Consulate are now confirmed team-wide weaknesses.
teamFocusItems.body: Oregon: 4 of 5 players below 50% with Sarge (28.6%/14M) and Hound (30%/10M) confirmed at scale. Consulate: all five players at 20–27% with 5–11M each — the most conclusive team-wide signal in the pool. Treat both as priority Ban 2 candidates. Villa is no longer a ban target — Grant (53.8%) and Peej (83.3%) are both clearly positive there.
